hub_subtitle: Business English for HR leadership in international meetings

lesson.menu_title: Something Was Happening — Past Continuous for Storytelling
lesson.menu_desc: Telling HR stories with the past continuous (was/were + verb-ing), when/while, and interruptions -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 18 -- Something Was Happening -- Past Continuous
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Murielle Xavier | Aula 18 -- Past Continuous

listenings.text: Let me tell you about yesterday morning. It was completely hectic. At nine, I was preparing the audit report when the system suddenly crashed. Meanwhile, the recruiters were interviewing candidates in the next room, and two meetings were running simultaneously. While I was calling IT, the auditors arrived unexpectedly. There was a real commotion in the corridor. Gradually, though, things were calming down. By eleven, the crisis was no longer unfolding, and everyone was smiling again. It was chaos, but we handled it as a team.

Good afternoon. I want to describe what was happening during the incident last week. It was a hectic day. While the team was running the onboarding session, a fire alarm went off unexpectedly. People were leaving the building, and meanwhile I was checking that everyone was safe. The situation was unfolding very fast. A few staff were still working when the alarm rang, so I was guiding them out. Gradually, the commotion was fading, and the auditors, who were visiting that day, stayed calm. Nobody was hurt, and by the afternoon we were back to normal.
